images.caption: Figure 1:TheMetal-Scibenchmark.Top:six optimization regimes (R1–R6), each stressing a structurally distinct GPU/memory bottleneck whose canonical recipe does not transfer to its neighbors (Sec.2).Bottom:the harness loop. A frozen LLMℳ\mathcal{M}emits a Metal sourceκk\kappa_{k}; the harness runtime-compiles it, dispatches across the in-distribution size configurationsΣ𝒯\Sigma_{\mathcal{T}}, and scores it against per-size roofline ceilings. The candidate becomes the new incumbentκk⋆\kappa^{\star}_{k}only whenS𝒯S_{\mathcal{T}}strictly improves. Compile diagnostics and per-size(χ𝒯,a𝒯)(\chi_{\mathcal{T}},\,a_{\mathcal{T}})flow back through a structured feedback packetℱk\mathcal{F}_{k}that primes the next iteration. The held-out evaluationΦ𝒯\Phi_{\mathcal{T}}atσ𝒯⋆\sigma^{\star}_{\mathcal{T}}runs once at end-of-run (Sec.3).

Figure 2:Per-task running self-speedup (best-so-far / seed) versus
iteration, Opus 4.7 (orange) vs Gemini 3.1 Pro (purple) vs GPT-5.5
(green). Filled circles mark the iteration that achieved the
final best; eachxalongy=1y{=}1is a candidate that
compiled or ran wrong (the incumbent is unchanged). The visible
counts make the silent-correctness story concrete: Opus emits more
failures than Gemini or GPT at every task with non-trivial
search (nbody,hmc,ising,lj,wave3d). The plot also justifies
the asymmetric per-task budgets: on most tasks (saxpy,heat2d,nbody,gradshaf,hmc,ising,lj) the incumbent stops moving by
iter∼\sim8 for all three models, butlbm’s Opus run plateaus at1.36×1.36\timesfrom iter 3 through iter 22 and only breaks through
to1.46×1.46\timesat iter 23 (the BGK fold + pinned threadgroup of
App.C);wave3d’s Opus run shows the same shape with
the lift at iter 14 (1.26×1.26\times). A 10-iter budget would have
missed both Opus exemplars. GPT’sfft3dclimbs monotonically to2.95×2.95\times, and is exactly the win that fails to
generalize on the held-out gate.
